name: Memorable Mincemeat
recipeInstructions:
Makes 8 cups (not 8 servings)
Combine all ingredients in a large saucepan. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Refrigerate or freeze in recipe size portions. Dark raisins may be used instead of currants, and ground cloves can substitute for mace. Processing mincemeat is recommended for long-term storage (1-2 years). Spoon into pint jars and process for 30 minutes in a boiling water bath.
Source: Festive Favourites, TransAlta Utilities Shared by Elizabeth Rodier, March 1993
From Blue Ribbon Cook Book, Winnipeg Manitoba 1905 - Mince Pie Hints. Dried apples soaked in water for a few hours can substitute for fresh apples in mince pies. Dried cherries and other fruits prepared with sugar can be soaked for 10-12 hours in a small amount of water, then both the water and fruit can be used instead of raisins. This can save money and still work well.
